The **2016 Brisbane Broncos season** is the 29th in [the club](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos")'s history. Coached by [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)"), and captained by [Corey Parker](/wiki/Corey_Parker_(rugby_league) "Corey Parker (rugby league)"), they compete in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League")'s [2016 Telstra Premiership](/wiki/2016_NRL_season "2016 NRL season"). Prior to the start of the Premiership season, the Broncos competed in the [2016 Auckland Nines](/wiki/2016_Auckland_Nines "2016 Auckland Nines"), finishing last in their pool. During the mid-season [2016 State of Origin series](/wiki/2016_State_of_Origin_series "2016 State of Origin series") six Broncos players were selected for Queensland.

The [NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/NRL_Auckland_Nines "NRL Auckland Nines") is a pre-season [rugby league nines](/wiki/Rugby_league_nines "Rugby league nines") competition featuring all 16 [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") clubs. The 2016 competition wias played over two days on 6 and 7 February at [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park"). The Broncos featured in the Hunua pool and played the [Warriors](/wiki/New_Zealand_Warriors "New Zealand Warriors"), [Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs") and [Sea Eagles](/wiki/Manly_Warringah_Sea_Eagles "Manly Warringah Sea Eagles").
